title: Mathematics
description: Numbers, ratios, fractions, and problem-solving adventures built for curious 5th graders.

title: English
description: Magical poems, vivid stories, vocabulary games, and grammar missions that make reading fun.

title: Science
description: Explore space, ecosystems, matter, and the human body through hands-on wonder and discovery.

title: Introduction to Ratios: The Cookie Rule!
description: Learn what a ratio is by sharing yummy cookies and candies using simple blocks!

children.text: Welcome to the Cookie Ratio World!
. A ratio helps us compare these two delicious items directly!
(read as '3 to 2'). This means for every 3 cookies, you will always have exactly 2 candies.

title: Simplifying Ratios: The Great Candy Sort!
description: Sort candies into equal groups and shrink big ratios down to their simplest form.

children.text: looks big, but both numbers share a factor of 4. Divide each side by 4 to get

title: Ratio Word Problems: Classroom Detective!
description: Solve real classroom puzzles using ratios — from art supplies to sports teams.

children.text: If the ratio of red pencils to blue pencils is 2:3 and there are 10 red pencils, each 'part' equals 5. Multiply to find blue pencils: 15!

title: Understanding Proportions: Scaling Up Patterns!
description: Discover how proportions help us expand recipes, scale drawings, and double cookie sizes!

children.text: If 1 glass of lemon juice needs 2 spoons of sugar (1:2), then 2 glasses need 4 spoons (2:4). Since 1:2 equals 2:4, they are in proportion!

title: Proportion Kitchen: Double the Smoothie!
description: Scale smoothie recipes up and down while keeping every flavor perfectly balanced.

children.text: To double a recipe, multiply every ingredient by the same number. That keeps the proportion — and the taste — just right!

title: Map Masters: Scale Drawings & Proportions
description: Use proportions to turn tiny map distances into real-world adventures across your town.

children.text: If 1 cm on a map equals 5 km in real life, a 3 cm line represents 15 km. Maps are proportions you can walk!

title: Fraction Pizza Party: Slices & Sharing
description: Cut pizzas into equal slices and discover numerators, denominators, and fair shares.

children.text: Cut a pizza into 8 equal slices. Eating 3 slices means you ate
of the whole pizza. Yum and math together!

title: Comparing Fractions: Who Got More Cake?
description: Use number lines and common denominators to compare fractions fairly.

children.text: by finding equivalent fractions with the same denominator. Bigger numerator wins!

title: Adding Fractions: Mixing Paint Colors
description: Blend fractional paint amounts and learn to add fractions with like denominators.

children.text: 1/5 + 2/5 = 3/5. You added 1 part plus 2 parts out of the same 5 total parts. Keep the denominator!

title: Multiplying Fractions: Mini Garden Plots
description: Find fractions of fractions when you plant flowers in only part of a garden bed.

children.text: Half of a quarter plot is 1/2 × 1/4 = 1/8 of the whole garden. Multiply tops, multiply bottoms!

title: Magical Rhythms: Reading and Understanding Poems
description: Read beautiful rhyming poems and learn how poets create magical music using pure words.

children.text:
The stars look down upon the sea,
As quiet and peaceful as can be.
They whisper secrets to the night,
And fill our sleepy dreams with light.
Spot the rhymes: sea/be and night/light. Rhyme gives poems their magical rhythm!

title: Paint with Words: Imagery in Poetry
description: See, hear, and feel poems through vivid sensory words that create pictures in your mind.

children.text: Golden sunlight danced on the rippling lake — you can SEE it! Strong verbs and sensory adjectives build imagery.

title: Write Your Own Magical Poem!
description: Draft a short rhyming poem about your favorite place using rhythm and repetition.

children.text: Pick a topic you love (treehouse, ocean, pet).
Choose a rhyme pattern: AABB or ABAB.
Read it aloud — does it sound musical?

title: Nouns & Verbs: Sentence Superheroes
description: Meet the naming heroes (nouns) and action heroes (verbs) that power every sentence.

children.text: In 'The dragon roars loudly,' dragon is the noun (who) and roars is the verb (does what).

title: Adjective Adventure: Describing Worlds
description: Add colorful adjectives to boring sentences and make your writing sparkle.

children.text: Change 'The dog ran' to 'The fluffy, joyful dog sprinted.' Adjectives tell us more about nouns!

title: Punctuation Power: Commas & More
description: Stop, pause, and shout with commas, periods, question marks, and exclamation points.

children.text: Use a comma in a list: 'I packed lunch, water, and a map.' Each mark tells readers how to breathe.

title: The Solar System: Journey Through Space!
description: Hop into our spaceship to explore the Sun, rocky planets, giant gas planets, and gravity!

children.text: sits at the center. Eight planets orbit it — four rocky inner worlds and four giant outer gas planets.

title: Planet Passport: Rocky vs. Gas Giants
description: Stamp your passport as you visit Mercury, Earth, Jupiter, and Neptune.

children.text: Rocky planets: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars — small and solid.
Gas giants: Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune — huge and stormy.

title: Moon Phases: Night Sky Calendar
description: Track the Moon from new to full and learn why its shape seems to change.

children.text: Why Does the Moon Change Shape?
We see sunlight bouncing off the Moon. As it orbits Earth, we see different portions lit — that's a phase!

title: Ecosystem Explorers: Food Chains
description: Follow energy from the Sun through plants, herbivores, and predators in a forest ecosystem.

children.text: Sun → grass → rabbit → fox. Each step is a link in the food chain. Energy flows, but never increases!

title: Habitats Around the World
description: Compare deserts, rainforests, and oceans — and the amazing animals adapted to each.

children.text: A habitat provides food, water, shelter, and space. Camels thrive in deserts; frogs love wetlands!

title: Ratio Basics
description: Discover what ratios are, how to write them, simplify them, and solve fun word problems.

title: Proportion Power
description: Scale recipes, drawings, and patterns using equal ratios and proportions.

title: Fraction Foundations
description: Slice, compare, and understand fractions with pizza, cake, and number lines.

title: Fraction Operations
description: Add and multiply fractions in real-world projects like art, cooking, and gardening.

title: Magical Poems
description: Read rhyming verse, explore imagery, and write your own enchanting poem.

title: Grammar Galaxy
description: Master nouns, verbs, adjectives, and punctuation to build super sentences.

title: Space & the Solar System
description: Blast off to learn about planets, the Moon, and our place in the Milky Way.

title: Ecosystems & Habitats
description: Trace food chains and explore how living things survive in different habitats.

title: Ratio & Proportion: Math Detective
description: Become a math detective! Crack ratio puzzles, simplify comparisons, and scale real-world recipes and maps using proportions. Perfect for 5th graders ready to think like investigators.

title: Fraction Fun Zone
description: From pizza slices to paint mixing, explore fractions with tasty examples and hands-on challenges. Compare, add, and multiply fractions while building number sense.

title: Magical Poems & Rhyme Time
description: Step into a world of rhythm and rhyme! Read enchanting poems, spot imagery, and write your own magical verses. A creative English adventure for young poets.

title: Grammar Galaxy
description: Launch into Grammar Galaxy! Meet nouns, verbs, adjectives, and punctuation heroes that power every great sentence. Short missions build confident writers.

title: Space & Nature Explorers
description: Roam the solar system and wander through forests! Study planets, moon phases, food chains, and habitats in one exciting science course for curious explorers.
